title: Bladder pain syndrome (BPS) - NHS
text:
wall) becoming irritated and inflamed. But it is unclear why this happens in some people.
It's thought it may be caused by:
your immune system mistakenly attacking your bladder wall, causing inflammation a problem with your bladder wall which makes it easily irritated by substances in your pee You are more likely to get BPS if you already have another long-term pain condition like  fibromyalgia .
Page last reviewed: 17 October 2025
Next review due: 17 October 2028
